source_docs.title: MARS-seq2.0: an experimental and analytical pipeline for indexed sorting combined with single-cell RNA sequencing

oligos.name: RT primer (RT1)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription / cell-barcoding primer; anneals to mRNA polyA and adds cell barcode, UMI, Illumina Read2 handle and T7 promoter

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter (core TAATACGACTCACTATAG; +1 GGG transcription start)

components.name: cell/well barcode
components.role: cell barcode (7 bp, X; edit distance >=2, ~constant GC)

components.name: anchored polyT
components.role: polyT RT primer with 3' anchor N

oligos.notes: Full 384-primer barcode list at compgenomics.weizmann.ac.il/tanay (Supplementary Table 1). Working concentration 12.5 nM; final in-reaction 4 nM.

oligos.name: Barcoded ligation adaptor (plate/pool adaptor)
oligos.role: RNA:ssDNA ligation adaptor added to 3' end of fragmented aRNA; encodes 4-bp pool/plate barcode + partial Read1 adaptor
oligos.notes: 5' phosphate (/5Phos/) enables ligation to RNA 3' end; 3' C3 spacer (/3SpC3/) blocks adaptor self-ligation. Full list in Supplementary Table 2. Stock 100 uM.

oligos.name: Second RT primer (RT2)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer complementary to the ligated plate adaptor
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of the adaptor AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAG; used at 25 uM.

oligos.name: Enrichment primer, forward (P5-Read1)
oligos.role: Final library PCR forward primer; adds Illumina P5 + TruSeq Read1 sequences on the pool-barcode side

oligos.name: Enrichment primer, reverse (P7-Read2)
oligos.role: Final library PCR reverse primer; adds Illumina P7 + Read2 sequences on the cell-barcode/UMI side
oligos.notes: No sample index used (libraries are pooled by the in-line 4-bp plate barcode; sequenced with no index reads).

oligos.name: Mouse ActB QC primer, forward
oligos.role: qPCR housekeeping-gene QC primer (mouse ActB 3' end)

oligos.name: Mouse ActB QC primer, reverse
oligos.role: qPCR housekeeping-gene QC primer (mouse ActB 3' end)

oligos.name: Human GAPDH QC primer, forward
oligos.role: qPCR housekeeping-gene QC primer (human GAPDH 3' end)

oligos.name: Human GAPDH QC primer, reverse
oligos.role: qPCR housekeeping-gene QC primer (human GAPDH 3' end)

final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC (P5) | TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT (TruSeq Read1/SP1) | NNNNN (5 nt random, ligation adaptor) | [SAMPLE_INDEX:4] (pool/plate barcode) | [CDNA] (3' mRNA-derived insert) | AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA (polyA) | [UMI:8] | [CELL_BARCODE:7] | AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC (Read2/SP2) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG (P7) -3'
annotation_lines: NNNNN: 5 random nt from the ligation adaptor (ignored in Read1)
[UMI:8]: unique molecular identifier, read in Read2
P7 flow-cell adaptor: ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG (rev-comp of CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT)

library_generation.title: FACS index-sort, lysis & RT1 annealing
library_generation.summary: Single cells are index-sorted into 384-well plates and lysed, and the well-specific RT1 poly-T primer anneals to the mRNA polyA tail.
library_generation.note: Each well contains a unique RT1 primer carrying a 7-bp cell barcode, 8-bp UMI, an Illumina Read2 handle and a T7 promoter; lysis buffer is evaporated and 0.5 ul RT mix added.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: SuperScript III extends RT1 to make first-strand cDNA tagged with the cell barcode, UMI, Read2 handle and T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: RT primer final concentration only 4 nM to minimize well-to-well contamination.

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I cleanup & pooling
library_generation.summary: Leftover RT primers are digested by exonuclease I in each well, then all 384 barcoded cells are pooled into one amplification batch and bead-purified.
library_generation.note: Pooling combines 384 wells into a single tube; QC1 qPCR estimates starting material.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: The pooled cDNA is converted to double-stranded DNA, making the T7 promoter double-stranded.
library_generation.note: NEBNext second-strand module (enzyme diluted 1:8) optimized to reduce PCR-contaminant noise.

library_generation.title: T7 in-vitro transcription (linear amplification)
library_generation.summary: T7 RNA polymerase linearly amplifies the dsDNA from the promoter into many antisense aRNA copies; template DNA is then DNase-digested.
library_generation.note: Amplified RNA (aRNA) carries the barcode/UMI/Read2 handle; QC2 measures IVT enrichment (~5 cycles).

library_generation.title: RNA fragmentation
library_generation.summary: aRNA is chemically fragmented with Zn2+ to a median size of ~300 nt.
library_generation.note: 3 min at 70 C; barcode-bearing 5' fragments retain the rd2/CB/UMI region.

library_generation.title: Ligation of plate (pool) barcode
library_generation.summary: The 5'-phosphorylated, 3'-blocked plate adaptor is ligated by T4 RNA ligase 1 to the 3' end of each fragmented aRNA, adding the 4-bp pool barcode.
library_generation.note: 3SpC3 block prevents adaptor concatemers; adaptor = /5Phos/[pool BC 4]NNNNN AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAG/3SpC3.

library_generation.title: Second reverse transcription (RT2)
library_generation.summary: RT2, complementary to the ligated adaptor, primes and reverse-transcribes the ligated aRNA into cDNA that now bears both barcodes.
library_generation.note: AffinityScript RT; 3 min 72 C anneal then extension.

library_generation.title: PCR enrichment (final library)
library_generation.summary: KAPA HiFi PCR with the P5-Read1 and P7-Read2 enrichment primers completes the Illumina adaptors and amplifies the final library.
library_generation.note: 11-17 cycles; final library peak 300-500 bp (mean ~384 bp). QC3 assesses final amount.

library_sequencing.note: Sequences from the Read1 handle into the pool-barcode side: first 5 nt are the random Ns of the ligation adaptor (ignored), the next 4 nt are the pool/plate (amplification-batch) barcode, then ~66 nt of 3'-end mRNA-derived cDNA (gene identity). No separate index reads are run.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer (SP1)
            5'-TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------------------->
top 5'-...ACAC TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNN BBBB CDNA(3')..... -3'
              ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||| ||||| ||||
bot 3'-...TGTG AGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA NNNNN VVVV cdna(3')..... -5'
                                            |_5N_| |pool| 3' mRNA (gene body)
                                           ignored  BC(4)   ---> 75 cycles

library_sequencing.note: Sequences from the Read2 handle back across the RT1-derived barcodes: reads the 7-bp cell/well barcode first, then the 8-bp UMI (15 cycles total). Sequencer parameters: rd1=75 bp, rd2=15 bp, no index reads (NextSeq 500, High Output v2).
library_sequencing.diagram:
top 5'-..CDNA-AAAA-[UMI 8]-[CB 7]-AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC..-3'
                               ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
bot 3'-..cdna-TTTT-[umi 8]-[cb 7]-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG..-5'
        <--------------------------  Read 2 primer (SP2)
      reads CB(7) then UMI(8)       5'-GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-3'
                                        ---> 15 cycles

title: MARS-seq2.0
description: MARS-seq2.0 is a plate-based, FACS-indexed, 3'-end single-cell RNA-seq method. Single cells are index-sorted into 384-well plates and reverse-transcribed with a poly-T primer carrying a 7-bp cell/well barcode, an 8-bp UMI, an Illumina Read2 handle and a T7 promoter. Pooled cDNA is made double-stranded, linearly amplified by T7 in-vitro transcription, fragmented, and given a 4-bp pool/plate barcode by RNA:ssDNA ligation; Illumina adaptors are then added by a second RT and PCR. Read1 captures the pool barcode plus the 3' mRNA sequence; Read2 captures the cell barcode and UMI.

reference.label: MARS-seq2.0: an experimental and analytical pipeline for indexed sorting combined with single-cell RNA sequencing

original_publication.title: MARS-seq2.0: an experimental and analytical pipeline for indexed sorting combined with single-cell RNA sequencing

throughput.summary: 8,000-10,000 cells sequenced per run; libraries of 20-40 384-well plates pooled per NextSeq 500 flow cell (~0.5x10^9 reads); >1 million cells processed overall.
throughput.rna: ~1,700 UMIs and ~700 genes per cell on average (range ~100-10,000 UMIs); saturation ~8,250 molecules/cell at 200,000 reads/cell
throughput.dna: Final library 1-10 ng/ul dsDNA, peak 300-500 bp (mean ~384 bp)

publication.statistical_model: Sequential-sampling/amplification model of RNA-seq data generation; spurious genomic positions ('offsets') filtered by a hypergeometric test (BH-adjusted); library complexity estimated by an exponential saturation fit; cell-to-cell contamination estimated from empty negative-control wells.

other.label: RT reaction volume
other.value: 500 nl (8-fold reduction vs original MARS-seq); RT primer 4 nM final

other.label: Cost / noise
other.value: ~$0.10 per cell; background noise reduced to ~2%

other.label: Sequencer / kit
other.value: Illumina NextSeq 500, High Output v2 (75 cycles); rd1=75 bp, rd2=15 bp, no indices

other.label: Data availability
other.value: GEO: GSE123392 (mouse ES and MEF example dataset)

other.label: Prior version
other.value: Builds on MARS-seq (Jaitin et al., Science 343:776-779, 2014)

data_processing.summary: Sequenced reads are run through the dedicated MARS-seq2.0 computational pipeline, which demultiplexes reads by their pool (plate) and cell (well) barcodes, error-corrects barcodes and UMIs, maps reads to the genome, and collapses UMIs into a per-gene molecule count per cell while modeling and subtracting background noise. It emits a per-amplification-batch diagnostics report with >20 QC metrics (reads, % exonic, UMIs, saturation) and estimates cell-to-cell contamination from empty negative-control wells.
data_processing.statistical_model: Sequential-sampling/amplification model of RNA-seq data generation; spurious genomic offsets filtered by a hypergeometric test (Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted); library complexity estimated by an exponential saturation fit
